boundary_rule_compliance.rule: Boundary Rule 2 — Components never read MLS state directly
boundary_rule_compliance.compliance: LeaveGroupButton reads live members only through context.getLiveMemberPubkeys(groupId). No marmot-ts reference anywhere in the component (verified: grep -c returns 0).

boundary_rule_compliance.rule: Boundary Rule 4 — marmot-ts import discipline (context imports dynamically, components not at all)
boundary_rule_compliance.compliance: getLiveMemberPubkeys uses await import('@internet-privacy/marmot-ts') inside MarmotContext.tsx, matching the context's existing ten dynamic-import sites. Zero static or dynamic marmot-ts import added to the component.

The pre-existing dead data-testid="leave-group-modal" on <Modal> was moved to <ModalContent>. The two new testids (abandon-group-notice, abandon-group-confirm-btn) sit on <Text>/<Button>. No <Modal> element in the file carries a data-testid after this diff.

getLiveMemberPubkeys.contract: getLiveMemberPubkeys(groupId) -> Promise<string[] | undefined>. Context method. Wraps getGroup-equivalent (client.groups.get, already null-safe via .catch(() => null)) + dynamic getGroupMembers. Resolves undefined — not [] — when unreadable.
getLiveMemberPubkeys.notes: Matches the seams table in architecture.md verbatim: three fail paths (no client, no mlsGroup, thrown during dynamic import/getGroupMembers) all resolve undefined; no path returns [].

selectLeaveModalState.contract: Pure, imported unchanged from S1's leaveEligibility.ts. LeaveGroupButton is now a pure consumer: selectLeaveModalState(liveMembers, adminPubkeys, ownPubkeyHex).
selectLeaveModalState.notes: No changes made to leaveEligibility.ts in this story — S1's module is consumed as-is.

order_sensitive_composition_notes.relevant_guarantee: Guarantee 2 (mixed freshness is deliberate): members are read live via getLiveMemberPubkeys on every modal open; adminPubkeys remains prop-derived, unchanged from before this story. This story does not touch how adminPubkeys reaches the component (still passed down from groups.tsx), preserving the deliberate staleness asymmetry architecture.md names.
order_sensitive_composition_notes.readiness_race: handleOpen() awaits getLiveMemberPubkeys(groupId) and calls setLiveMembers(members) BEFORE calling onOpen(). Because React 18 batches both state updates from the same async continuation, the modal never mounts with an earlier liveMembers value — the live read is guaranteed resolved before selectLeaveModalState is evaluated for the render that shows the modal.
order_sensitive_composition_notes.fail_closed_direction: Confirmed opposite-direction fail-closed default preserved: this story's read defaults to undefined -> isLastMember false -> never 'abandon'. S3's leaveGroupImpl (not yet implemented) will independently default the opposite way (no mlsGroup -> send anyway) per architecture.md's table — no shared resolver was built, consistent with critical guard R2 from the story brief.
